text: Use select mode for nodes and canvas objects.
Use hand mode to pan the canvas.

Nexus temporary tooling drive

Why this drive exists
Nexus creates a temporary drive alias only while setup tools such as Python, Git, pip, or archive extraction are running.
The alias keeps Windows paths short enough for those tools to work reliably.

Normal behavior
The drive disappears automatically after the current Nexus setup, repair, update, or installation task completes.
No action is needed while Nexus is running.

If this drive remains after Nexus has closed or crashed
1. Confirm that Nexus for ComfyUI is no longer running.
2. Double-click UNMOUNT_THIS_NEXUS_DRIVE.cmd from this folder.
3. Confirm the Windows prompt only when you want to remove the drive.

Safety
The unmount script removes only temporary drive aliases that point to this exact Nexus runtime folder.
It does not remove unrelated drives or user-created mappings.
